From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Canadian and American dramatic soprano

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/57/Othalie2020.jpg/250px-Othalie2020.jpg)](/wiki/File:Othalie2020.jpg)

Othalie Graham

**Othalie Graham** (born 1977) is a Canadian and American [dramatic soprano](/wiki/Dramatic_soprano "Dramatic soprano"), known for operatic roles such as Turandot in *[Turandot](/wiki/Turandot "Turandot")*, Tosca in *[Tosca](/wiki/Tosca "Tosca")*, Minnie in *[La fanciulla del West](/wiki/La_fanciulla_del_West "La fanciulla del West")*, Aida in *[Aida](/wiki/Aida "Aida")*, Elektra in *[Elektra](/wiki/Elektra_(opera) "Elektra (opera)")* and Ariadne in *[Ariadne Auf Naxos](/wiki/Ariadne_auf_Naxos "Ariadne auf Naxos")*.

## Early years

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Othalie_Graham&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early years")]

Graham was born in [Brampton](/wiki/Brampton "Brampton"), Ontario.[[1]](#cite_note-Shengold-1) Her father, a [Jamaican](/wiki/Jamaica "Jamaica")-born Canadian, instilled in Graham a strong identification with Jamaican music and culture.[[1]](#cite_note-Shengold-1) When Graham was young, her father sparked her interest in opera by taking her to see a [Leontyne Price](/wiki/Leontyne_Price "Leontyne Price") recital.[[1]](#cite_note-Shengold-1) Her passion in opera grew while attending high school at the [Etobicoke School of the Arts](/wiki/Etobicoke_School_of_the_Arts "Etobicoke School of the Arts"). While in Canada, Graham was awarded first place in the Jeunes Ambassadeurs Lyriques Competition and received the coveted Jean Chalmers prize in the Canadian Music Competition.[[2]](#cite_note-Festival-2)

Graham attended the [Academy of Vocal Arts](/wiki/Academy_of_Vocal_Arts "Academy of Vocal Arts")[[3]](#cite_note-masterwork-3) after studying under Lois McDonall.[[4]](#cite_note-4) At AVA, she studied under [Bill Schuman](/w/index.php?title=Bill_Schuman&action=edit&redlink=1 "Bill Schuman (page does not exist)") and continues to study with him.[[1]](#cite_note-Shengold-1) Graham has won the Opera at Florham voice competition[[5]](#cite_note-5) as well as the [Liederkranz Foundations](/wiki/Liederkranz_of_the_City_of_New_York "Liederkranz of the City of New York")'s annual competition,[[6]](#cite_note-6) where she made her [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") [recital](/wiki/Concert#Recital "Concert") debut.[[7]](#cite_note-7) She was also the New Jersey district winner of the Metropolitan Opera National Council Auditions and a finalist in the Palm Beach Opera Competition.[[2]](#cite_note-Festival-2) Graham also spent a summer training under the widely admired [Italian](/wiki/Italy "Italy") [opera](/wiki/Opera "Opera") singer [Renata Scotto](/wiki/Renata_Scotto "Renata Scotto") at the Renata Scotto Opera Academy at the Music Conservatory of Westchester.[[2]](#cite_note-Festival-2)[[3]](#cite_note-masterwork-3)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Othalie_Graham&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8f/Othalie_Graham_2.jpg/250px-Othalie_Graham_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Othalie_Graham_2.jpg)

Othalie Graham

Graham started her professional career in October 2004 in the title role of [Puccini](/wiki/Puccini "Puccini")'s [Turandot](/wiki/Turandot "Turandot") at [OperaDelaware](/wiki/OperaDelaware "OperaDelaware"),[[8]](#cite_note-perfectly-8) a roll in which she repeated at the [Utah Festival Opera](/wiki/Utah_Festival_Opera "Utah Festival Opera").[[9]](#cite_note-9) Her performance was well received, with one reviewer writing she "has a huge, powerful voice that fills the auditorium and navigates the musical difficulties beautifully"[[10]](#cite_note-10) another writing, "a statuesque soprano with both power and sweetness in a disciplined voice."[[11]](#cite_note-11)

In 2005, she was awarded the prestigious Sullivan Foundation Grant.[[2]](#cite_note-Festival-2)

In 2006, her performance in the production of "Turandot" at [the Sacramento Opera](/wiki/Sacramento,_California#Culture_and_arts "Sacramento, California") was identified as the single reason that its success was never in question.[[12]](#cite_note-12) In July 2006, Graham added title role of Floria Tosca in [Giacomo Puccini](/wiki/Giacomo_Puccini "Giacomo Puccini")'s opera *[Tosca](/wiki/Tosca "Tosca").*[[3]](#cite_note-masterwork-3)

In 2007, Graham added the role of Odabella in [Attila](/wiki/Attila_(opera) "Attila (opera)") as part of her [Sarasota Opera](/wiki/Sarasota_Opera "Sarasota Opera") debut, where she "blasted Young-Bok Kim's Attila off his throne" with "a big wagnerian voice."[[13]](#cite_note-13)

On September 25, 2007, Her performance of "Aida" at [El Paso](/wiki/El_Paso "El Paso") [Opera House](/wiki/Opera_House "Opera House") was amazing having critic [Paul Westman](/w/index.php?title=Paul_Westman&action=edit&redlink=1 "Paul Westman (page does not exist)") saying "At the end of the scene I was blown out of my seat by the loudest E flat that I have ever heard in my life! The interesting thing is that the note did not "sound HIGH" to my ear as it usually does at end of a cabaletta. It was huge and cut through the opera house like a laser beam!".[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Othalie_Graham&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Awards")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This section of a [biography of a living person](/wiki/Wikipedia:Biographies_of_living_persons "Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons") **does not [include](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [references or sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help by adding [reliable sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources"). Contentious material about living persons that is unsourced or poorly sourced **must be removed immediately**, especially if potentially [libelous](/wiki/Wikipedia:Libel "Wikipedia:Libel") or harmful. *(March 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

* First place in the Jeunes Ambassadeurs Lyriques Competition
* Jean Chalmers prize in the Canadian Music Competition
* Winner of Edward Johnson Competition
* Winner of Opera at Florham Voice Competition
* Winner of Liederkranz Society Competition in Wagner division
* New Jersey district winner of the Metropolitan Opera National Council Auditions
* Finalist in Palm Beach Opera Competition
* Sullivan Foundation Grant recipient
* First place winner in the Gerda Lissner International Vocal Competition in Wagner Division
* First place winner of the Joyce Dutka Competition
